channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems — 93 replies — 372.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-06-13 15:01 UTC

OP

Kek fudniggers

Early replies (first 3)

62354945
>Inb4 source
https://www.icmagroup.org/assets/documents/Regulatory/Repo/ICMA-report-DLT-and-repo-part-I-June-2026-040626.pdf

62354971
it's all so tiring
i have sold much more of my stack than i wanted assuming that price would react by now

62354976
>>62354943
Cool, when will I be rich?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359988
>>62359501
>I have gone through so much turmoil

look at this kid talking about how hard it was to sit at a computer and read buzz words
your grandfather would be disgusted

> and learning while holding this damned token

bro you haven't learned shit, except that a fool and his money are soon parted

62360040
>>62359988
yes link marines like to pretend they are on the samurai warrior's mystical journey of self-discovery or something lmao

62360066
>>62354976
When the "antichrist" system comes into effect, I guess.
Assuming you survive whatever they have planned between now and then.

62360120
>>62360040
yeah it's pathetic


i would pay to see some of these boyscouts give a live presentation in their own words about one of those infographics like the one in op.
the chainlink boyscout revels in the fact that their cult investment comes off as so high iq that no one can understand it, including themselves if they're being honest.

the position for them is essentially a vanity hold, where they can fantasize about the day they show the world their misunderstood, off the charts intelligence. the problem is of course, that day isn't real

their hubris has been quite costly over the years

62360137
>>62359988
>>62360040
Ill pray for you two (probably one person on multiple ID’s)

62360230
>>62360137
we don't need your prayers stinkbag
we're doing just fine in the material realm without the assistance of your supposed "son of God" who was born in "immaculate conception" (lmao cope for Mary being a whore)

62360247
>>62355053
Three more years.... then my real life can begin

62360331
>>62360230
Im praying still

62360405
>>62360331
well stop

62360577
>>62355053
Yjk this nigger typed 2 more years but decided to throw an extra year in there because it would be too on the nose. Evil fucking nigger kek

62360584
>>62355583
>Flat feet bug gf poster is back
>Link down another -30% since
Sorry bro you better get used to the flapjack feets

62360663
>>62354976
did you take the vax?

62360802
>>62360066
What if link is actually worth something but you can't use it to buy bread. Like kinda like if you had an entire continent in your name but you still need the knowledge to extract resources, work with local governing bodies etc etc

Basically what if we have tokens but are unable to use them because we're goyim

62360825
>>62360137
>duuuuuude it's all a consssssssthhhhpirathy!!!

there he is everyone, our very own sleuth

excuse me, DETECTIVE sleuth

nothing, and i mean NOTHING, gets by this guy. he can sniff out colluding posts in seconds. this guy is GOOD, and i feel ashamed at ever trying to get one by him

afterall, everyone knows that there's nothing more important than guarding the reputation of a dead altcoin on a dead forum


well, you know what we say here...

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62360858
>>62360802
Well, then we're fucked either way.

62360859
>>62360825
>>62360405
Jesus loves you

62360865
>>62360859
religion is the opiate of the masses
or in this case, the stink marines

62360873
>>62360865
That doesn’t change that fact that I don’t hate you and I forgive you.

62360876
>>62360873
your "forgiveness" is rejected, stinkie
how does that make you feel

62361417
>>62360876
feels to me like you're gonna take my whole cock in your ass, and you're gonna like it.

62361431
>>62354976
2030

This year was always the plan for chainlink

62361469
>>62360876
Lilla My, why do you have so much hatred for stinkies?

62361506
I thought green man sergay was going to make me rich...

62361627
>>62358895
COPE

62361699
>>62354943
7 years
7 dollars

62361781
>>62361773

62362456
it's very easy, either it works or I'll keep my stack as a lesson in humility

there is no scenario where I sell

62362461
>>62362456
cool beta attitude, stinker
just admit you lost the game of el lifio

62363300
>>62359501
>>62360137
>>62360331
Jesus is the way truth and life but link is equivalent to the money changer parasites he flipped the tables on who ultimately lose. Invest accordingly.

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 52 replies — 208.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-13 15:06 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362021
>>62361542
Warren Buffett: owns companies that provide goods and services people actually buy.
Elon Musk: tells retards he's going to Mars and he assumes this shit that's never going happen, is worth trillions in discounted future earnings. Meanwhile his actual IRL companies don't even make any money after being around for 20 years.

He is a scammer and you are his paypig. Congratulations.

62362038
>>62361499
>But it is now the future, and they are performing poorly, the BYD makes superior electric vehicles,
Yeah that's another thing. His EV company has been defeated by China. What happens when China starts a space company. Will his paypigs still keep bidding up his unprofitable companies for no reason, or will they become China fans instead?

62362068
>>62361478
the space industry was flailing on its last shitdick before spacex came into the picture. i hate him and his companies, but that much is true. he invigorated people and showed them that private space flight was not only possible, but that it could attract even government contracts. as for tesla, he put electric cars on the global market at a bigger scale than ever and shifted public attitude towards them. you have to understand the cultural context his companies came out of.

62362141
>>62361966
>Belle Delphine is technically a trillionaire too since she has an unlimited supply of bath water
now, this is analology

62362170
>>62361515
If you live outside of the US, you can see with your own eyes the meteoric rise of Chinese vehicles.

62362179
>>62361452
The greater fool game.
One day the bubble will not just pop it'll burst.

62362395
>>62361452
people don't want to buy shit that makes their lives marginally better

a better toaster or a nicer laptop or an AI chat tool is not meaningfully changing people's lives

you know what does? a dream for a future where we are on mars and doing cool space shit

62362428
>>62362395
>a dream for a future where we are on mars and doing cool space shit
That's literally nothing. I have a plan to get us to Andromeda galaxy. Give me $10T right now.

62362436
>>62362428
I mean, if you had big ass space craft and thousands of engineers and a plan, maybe yea you could get $10T. But you don't.

62362441
>>62362436
>I mean, if you had big ass space craft and thousands of engineers and a plan, maybe yea you could get $10T. But you don't.
He doesn't have enough of that stuff to get a man to mars ether, yet still he gets a trillion for owning the least profitable large cap tech stocks in America.

62362449
>>62362441
you can read the OP in my thread here: >>62362383
spaceX in their own documents aren't saying they believe in space exploration
that's just the story they are selling the the goys and retail investors

spaceX plans to make its money on building data centers, just like anthropic, oracle, openai, google, literally every other tech company right now
and again, to be clear, I agree with you this is stupid and this bubble will eventually pop. or maybe it won't. the dotcom bubble never really popped, the market just kept growing and software really was that valuable.

anyways. the stories Musk tells about space exploration are mainly marketing to get dumb people and X followers excited, he has already realized he can't deliver on that goal. the big picture for spaceX doesn't involve space exploration at all.

62362464
>>62362449
>the dotcom bubble never really popped
Get a load of this baggie.

62362537
>>62361452
Go back to rebbit, faggot.

62362557
>>62361478
>He
He doesn't do shit in his companies

62362583
He's ran a very good public relations campaign the last few decades. He made sure that his name was associated with his products. Tesla is synonymous with Elon Musk

62362589
>>62361452
Guy I'm stuck in e13 figure hell and people are making fun of me online. What do?

62362674
>>62362449
maybe the data center angle is just what they are selling to the institutional investors, and they really do believe in space exploration?

62362684
>>62361478
>robotics
he hasn't sold a single robot

62362688
>>62362068
he received massive subsidies the entire time in tesla
That's not his doing. Politicians wanted EVs no matter if he produced them or someone else did
They were already was creating a car before he joined Tesla

62362689
>>62361452
Of all the famous billionaires, Musk's companies are the only truly innovative ones. Like many of us, I don't like his liberalism or his transhumanism, but it's undeniable that the "hype" that fuels his investments is based on the significant chance that he can pull of what he says. Afterall everyone wrote off starlink and autonomous cars.

I do find it irritating how people can't talk about him or spacex in good faith. They hide behind investment language they don't understand when the truth is that they hate him and his companies because the media told them to. The regime media has had it in for him ever since he bought twitter since they had a near total monopoly on information before that point. You're seeing just how much they hate the situation by the fact that the UK grooming gang discussion in parliament was hijacked by deranged leftists into a 2 minute hate against Musk.
These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example, a truly hideous "person" who has done nothing except harm humanity and steal money.

62362700
>>62361452
Now that he's a trilly, do you goys think this bitch will still settle for that $500k/year he was giving her or will that 10x soon?

62362803
>>62362689
>they hate him and his companies because the media told them to
I simply hate all billionaires because it's fucked

62362809
>>62362689
>The regime media
the ones who pushed EVs?
>These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example
you mean the currency manipulator who "broke the bank of england" ?
who did he steal money from?

62362814
>>62361452
In a sea of grifting the guy offers at least a unique perspective on his multiple scams. For that he is awarded 1 trillion dollars

62362818
>>62361452
ask yourself which space sector company can dislodge SpaceX from government contract welfare. they are now too embedded, much like Microsoft with their shitty products, and will always recieve enough taxpayer gibs to keep them profitable while also paying investors. literally nobody else can compete

62362863
>>62361452
It's a mystery

62362883
>>62361478
He makes promises he can't keep and retards like you fall for it every time which keep the stocks inflated.

62362985
>>62361452
It's paper networth. His net worth is in stock shares. The stock market has nothing to do with real life and is just a popularity contest. Buying a share of Tesla and thinking you own a piece of Tesla despite it never paying a dividend or doing a buyback is no different than buying dodgecoin and thinking u own a stake in the dog. His stocks only value is just what ever u can tell to for to the next person. All he does is pull from science fiction and that keeps his stock popular. It's literally no different from a casino. Ultimately this gambling money comes from the petro dollar

62363104
>>62362557
Poorfag doesn't bussines

62363313
>>62361452
People are so impressed by the self-landing rockets and the sci fi visions that they throw their money at him without any thought on whether the numbers are reasonable.

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 87 replies — 138.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-06-13 14:29 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349408
>>62342798
bro you're onto something.
Fake accounts FLOOD the internet. Especially kinky shit like fetlife and the kink subreddits - they will have the exact same contact for totally different account and profiles.
I found 2 accounts that had each other as friends.. they had similar profiles, said they "knew each other online" and 1 used a keyword that the other one was talking to me and using..
there is a MASSIVE psyop happening.

62349422
>>62342700
OP, you clearly haven't heard this enough, you're a faggot.

62351731
>>62346960
everything that you post (on an almost daily basis) that never actually happened outside your autistic mongoloid mind, champ. I love these stories about the "middle class" lol. Keep up the good work soldier. thanks for the laughs (unironically speaking)

62354799
>>62342905
Elliot only drove a BMW because his dad had some cushy Hollywood job working on films like Hunger Games, etc. Pretty sure he wrote something in his manifesto seething about seeing some guy driving a shitbox and still having a beautiful girl in the passenger seat too.

62354802
>>62342834
ha, i was thinking this read exactly like that post

62354821
>>62342828
When they're writing long form fiction they get a pass.

62355262
>>62342700
Electrical contractor here. I live closer to the beach than you do. It’s kind of funny, most skilled tradesmen look down on people like yourself. We feel sorry for you because in the grand scheme of things - you’re worthless. Unable to build or fix anything. Totally dependent on others. Kinda pathetic

62355284
>>62355262
The mind of a blue collar is truly pathetic.

62355301
>>62342770
I haven’t worked in 1,5 months and i can already feel myself wanting to just become passive and not really being impressed or motivated by anything (brain getting fried by dopamine and serotonine). You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.

62355307
>>62355301
You’re pathetic. Since a little kid, all the classmates of my have been retired.

62355313
>>62355301
> You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
You’re probably just poor. People making a top 10% income from investments tend not to work.

62355322
>>62355301
>1.5 months
You don’t even have a non working lifestyle built yet.

62355326
>>62355307
This may be the funniest claim I've seen you make. Absolutely nobody from your school does any kind of work at all? Most people do find things to keep themselves occupied with, beyond mere shitposting on an anonymous uzbekistani finger painting notification board.

62355338
>>62355326
Maybe we grew up in different classes.
On one hand, /biz/ loves to larp as rich and successful and on the other hand, loves to larp as muh productive and I don’t like doing nothing.
Fucking jokes.
I hear people say what you say all the time. Yet look at retired people, I don’t see you saying shit to them. Yet when it is someone young and not working, suddenly they are “unproductive.” Even though these people are clearly rich.

62355342
>>62355307
>>62355313
>>62355322
Kek did i trigger you too much?

62355360
>>62355342
I have never in my life looked at someone not working and thought anything bad.
Growing up, my dad retired when I was like 5. By 10, the parents of all my school friends were retired long ago.
By past college, the differences between the haves and have nots became noticeable because college is where you truly see class differences because high school and under tend to mainly kids from your neighborhood.
You cannot imagine life without work yet kids that grew up like me were surrounded by adults that don’t work. It is the normal. People like me just carry on the lifestyle.
People from successful families don’t break their back.

62355375
>>62355301
Depends what you mean by work. But any occupation today is not a good use of time. Like work as in what? Something you need to get a college degree for? White collar?
Or something blue collar? That’s even dumber.
Starting your own business? Okay sure, if you want to start it as a hobby..?
I don’t know. People build lifestyles when they don’t need to work to live.
Some travel, some have many social gatherings
>Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
Oh yeah, the bottom 90% definitely feel alive struggling lol.
Struggling for what? Lmao.

62355404
>>62355301
Sometimes I see the kids in my neighborhood. This isnt an ultra rich 100m a year neighborhood or millions a month neighborhood but in 10-20 years when these kids grow up, it would be a waste of time for them to get a “job.”
Get it?

62357658
>>62355301
Rabbis rabbiposting as usual. Nice try but no, i won't work for (((you)))

62359024
>>62342770
I have 300k...where should i buy a house and get on gibbs? Caliwali?

62359462
>>62342700
I mean someone has to like do stuff u need someone to actually do stuff

62359491
>>62342770
This is called capitalism btw. You either exploit others or u work until you die. Hello knock knock HELLO this is basic capitalism. Wake up everything u guys complain about is due to market forces the Jews are a fantasy of cohesion to keep your little world simple bigga

62360989
Never worked a day in my life and I would never shit on a trade they keep my water running and the electricity flowing. Would you trust a 3rd worlder to wire up your outlet in your house and not expect to die in your sleep when that shit catches fire fuck no I'm going to pay a white American 150$ an hour to do that shit right. Without their goods and services our money doesn't mean shit and I might aswell get raped by a pack of niggers.

62361159
All work is automateable. No one of you should feel safe.

62361494
>>62344611
>the producing members
The secret is these types wouldnt know what to do in life other than work. Their life centers around it. You're supposed to exploit this in them

62363059
>>62342700
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62363064
>>62361159
What about work on new products that have never been fully before
Someone has to build it first for a robot to even try

62363066
>>62360989
Based

62363074
>>62342905
BMW stock is worth about $25 kek

62363190
>>62361494
Yeah.
Half of Americans own less than 200k in networth but these people try to call me a loser cause I don’t need work.

item.title: Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-13 14:56 UTC

OP

Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.

https://worldofclaudecraft.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62362742
oldschool runescape is free

62362747
looks like shit
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.
sure if you can scam some subhumans but you're competing with 1 billion jeet scammers

62362749
>>62362737
Buy
An
Ad
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362753
>>62362737
reminds me of the good old days.

62362775
4 years into the AI era and it still hasn't produced a single good or useful thing.

62362806
>>62362737
I am an europoor and drumpf blocked my access to fable. How can I compete?

62362843
The overall background changes the further I walk away.
Now it's broken.

62362844
AND it's gone

62362866
>>62362843
>>62362844
kek

62362946
>finally get it to work
>say nigger in the chat
>get banned

kek

62363056
AI reminds me of peak crypto mania yet somehow even more cringe

62363065
>>62362806
Pick your turnips and shut the fuck up.

62363118
>>62362946
works for me

62363279
Janky but cute. Call me in 10 years

item.title: its not fair — 13 replies — 36.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
its not fair

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-13 14:45 UTC

OP

>there are people right now
>yes this very second
>flying on private jets to dubai, St barths
>and fucking 10/10 giga slut IG baddies

https://www.tiktok.com/@luxuryexplorer1/video/7459430622960962848?is_from_webapp=1&sender_device=pc

Early replies (first 3)

62363142
>>62363136
I am so jelous ir sometimhing, financially.

62363143
>>62363142
ITS NOT FAIR NIGGA I DESERVE TO FLY ON PRIVATE JETS AND DRIVE LAMBO SVJS AND FUCK HOT IG SLUTS

I CANT HANDLE BEING A POOR INCEL ANYMORE IM LOSING MY MIND

62363151
>>62363143
you're living your life through a screen lmao. you're gonna be on your deathbed like
>"OH MY NIGGER I WISH I SPENT MORE TIME THINKING ABOUT OTHER PEOPLES' LIVES"

bro you are fucking dumb for feeling bad that other's appear to have something you dont, for the rest of humanity everybody will experience things that others don't. it makes no sense to stress about it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363155
>>62363136
>open video
>negroe muttering noises
disgusting

62363156
>>62363151
sorry elon becoming a trillionaire yesterday made me feel some type of way ngl

62363158
I dont give a fuck about visiting some sandnigger shithole, I just dont wanna work anymore.

62363162
>>62363143
Touch grass then.

62363164
>>62363162
Working on it i downloaded that meetup app and am going to an event this tuesday.

62363175
>>62363156
bro there's people who have life set by birth to be in grandeur luxury without even having done any work. if it wasn't elon or luxuryexplorer1 or whatever; it would be someone else. you're just not ignorant of it, just learn to fucking cope with it; it will take a while but you'll grow up. I can tell you're under the age of 20,

62363182
>>62363164
Good. This is good anon, tryout https://timeleft.com/ too. It's honestly really good, it's like omegle in real life. Attracts an extroverted crowd, you'll like it.

62363187
>>62363158
this
let me sit at home play vidya and smoke weed, nothing in life is better than being left alone.

62363204
>>62363187
Frens and famiky are good sinetines, but yeah, fuck the posing gucciniggers!

62363251
>>there are people right now
sadly this is true

item.title: SPCX is the new bitcoin. — 11 replies — 22.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
SPCX is the new bitcoin.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-13 14:37 UTC

OP

SPCX is the new bitcoin.

Early replies (first 3)

62362526
>>62362506
You honestly might be right

62362529
>>62362506
So it's finna crash hard?

62362543
Nah nah nah, the tam is saying each share is worth $22,000. We are in chipotle hours now!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362551
I'll give you my DD.

I was watching Bloomberg this entire week. Every single day they avoided talking about SPCX. Everytime it was brought up, they said it was worth nothing.

Even early in the morning the day of the IPO, they kind of didn't talk about it until open. They didn't want to show Elon playing rocket man and so on. I kept watching Bloomberg and then something weird started happening. The hosts started getting really angry and salty. They kept bring up blue origin as a threat if they ever IPO'd and wanted to not talk about spacex anymore.

I continued to watch and eventually interviews started coming in where boombers were angry that they didn't get shares allocated to them and this Business Professor at the duke university got red in the face and said he was going to tell his university MBA students about this sham of an IPO. He rose up 1 finger and said "that's all I got, 1 share!"

That's when I knew this was the new bitcoin. It is born out of butthurt.

62362914
Mark my words. SPCX is going to $80k.

62362917
They hold bitcoin on the balance sheet

62362933
>>62362506
yep, -70% is programmed

62362934
>>62362506
No it's not

62363082
>>62362914

full psychosis in effect

>>62363082

item.title: ETH ETH Txn cost on L1 is $0.004 . Txns at all time high — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETH ETH Txn cost on L1 is $0.004 . Txns at all time high

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-13 15:05 UTC

OP

ETH L1 has already scaled drastically. People were complaining that ETH costs are way too high

Now that it's solved, why isn't it reflecting in price? Even after Tom Lee bought close to 5% of the supply, nothing happens to the price

Early replies (first 3)

62363245
Where else in the world can you transact peer to peer without middleman for $0.004 and the transactions are settled within seconds?

62363249
And why the f is BTC with zero use case at $1.2T market cap and ETH so low?
This doesn't make any sense

62363253
>>62363240
Twas never about high fees, it was almost this low for 2 years, even at 5k price. Twas about kinder maxikings and le ethkiller kumar shills fudding it, imagine posting here that vb and ef sold 100k eth in the orev year when lee boat it in the past week alone.... Basterd!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363262
>>62363245
L2s were a fail, i told it 5 years ago thats not the way, finally the nigger vitaleek admitted it a few months ago. Now we only need a mania and it ll pump another few dollars again, but yet i am buying under 1500 for now.

62363311
>>62363249
BTC is expensive because BTC is expensive. It gets all the attention of crypto because it's the biggest and oldest. Partly the same reason gold is much more valuable than other metals. ETH does the same SoV as BTC (and arguably does it better) but because it gets less attention, it gets less buys. Also Bitcoin doesn't have competing smart contract platforms launching constant smear campaigns against it. Maybe Bcash but that was almost a decade ago.

item.title: Elon is going to build a data center dAIson sphere around the sun — 21 replies — 13.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Elon is going to build a data center dAIson sphere around the sun

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-13 13:31 UTC

OP

Buy space exploration technologies company stock BUY BUY BUY

Early replies (first 3)

62359811
>>62359800
kek

62359825
>>62359800
will that thing cast a shadow on my backyard? my petunias need the sunlight

62359829
>>62359800
This may be controversial but I think we will see a Dyson Sphere within our lives.

Technology is exponential. Look how long we spent in the Dark Ages with basic mathematics and science then we hit the industrial revolution and in a few hundred of years we are here.

Now with the AI revolution things will happen even faster. Technology enables us to do things faster, better, and more efficiently. With AI we will be travelling between planets regularly in just a few decades.

It might sound crazy but so would a smart phone 200 years ago.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359836
>>62359829
I hope not, it's terribly ugly. Futurists really don't seem to have any aesthetic sense whatsoever. What point is there in traveling to other planets if the people going there are hideous and uglify everything they touch?

62359837
When is Elon going to build a Mass Relay to the Citadel? I need some blue Asari ass

62359841
>space exploration technologies

62359842
>>62359829
Unless we get real Artificial Superintelligence soon you can forget about that.

62360179
>>62359841
inaneh compootah

62360249
>>62359800
Yes.

62360253
EDS trannies will literally say this is fake, but it's real and it's happening. 10,000 Starship launches per year by 2028, the dyson sphere is happening

62360297
>>62359800
kek

62360306
>>62359800
By 2027 none the less!!!!

62360337
They will build special space radiators of carbon and shit and lasers

62360362
>>62359800
Who's gonna build it? Mexicans?

62360396
>>62359836
its not about beauty, its about turning energy into compute

62360433
>>62359836
How often do you look at the sun

62360446
>>62360362
AI ROBOTS

62360616
>>62359829
Absolutely no chance. A Dyson sphere isn't a single monolithic structure, it's a series of maybe trillions of separate space habitats, some connected together as islands and archipelagos, and many (but not all) spinning for artificial gravity.
It isn't built as one big project, it grows organically as the population it sustains increases. A Dyson sphere can support somewhere in the realm of 50 quintillion people, and our population increases roughly 1-3% per year. Even if you account for people intentionally reproducing much more frequently and living longer, the need for a full Dyson sphere is still a really, really long way away.

I think it is plausible but unlikely that we could build a rotating space habitat (O'Neill cylinder) in an L4 or L5 solar orbit in our lifetimes. But it will largely come down to real estate prices. Once it's cheaper to build in space than to buy land on Earth, we'll start doing it. There are a lot of key technology steps we're waiting on to make it affordable. Mining the asteroid belt is a big one, but I argue that the biggest problem is launch costs. Once we have a more efficient way to get things into space, the cost of building up there drops enormously. Probably the most feasible option right now is a tethered ring space launcher like from Project Atlantis, but it still has a lot of problems. One day we will likely have a series of orbital rings around the Earth which make getting up into orbit as easy and cheap as catching a train.

But don't misunderstand me, the future of humanity is absolutely in space. It's almost certain that one day the supermajority of humanity will live in rotating space habitats rather than on planets.

62363032
>>62359800
He's trying to get to mars which is much closer and in the opposite direction. A dyson sphere the size of the sun would not be possible there isn't enough material available on our planet to construct even half of a ring.

62363034
>>62359825
We'll build it just beyond Mars' orbit, so only outer planets shitters will be caught in the shade

62363039
The solar datacenter could power everything we need. Youtube, facebook, and x. It's literally free.

62350592

62350594
>>62350565
Except fin is a tranny drug poison. Post finasteride syndrome reddit has 15k unique weekly visits.

62350624
>>62350594
About 20% of men between 18-35 (who arent on fin) develop ED.

When people who do take fin develop ED, of course the drug that nukes users dicks (temporarily) 1% of the time is what they'll blame it on, while in reality it's something else entirely. I'd bet the number of finasteride users who develop permanent ED is pretty close to 20%, the same percentage as men who don't use it.

62350707
>>62349989
Just make it socially acceptable to wear hats everywhere. I am balding and I just wear a hat all the time and I was still able to get laid. Basically by the time I make it to the point the hat comes off it is too late and the sex happens anyway. I'd imagine this is what it is like when trans women trick straight men into bed with them.

62350754
>>62350707

62350920
>>62349989
attractive men = baby boom

62351695

everyone looks like kpop
big orgy ensues

62351707
>>62350594
Finasteride has a half life of 6 hours and PFS is a fake condition invented by American grifters and bald cucks.

62351757
>>62349989
good work mr president, that outta improve the birth rate

62352443
>>62350565
20 a year? I pay 9 a month
t. eufag

62352757
>>62352443
I’m eu too and I get a 98-pack of 5mg for about €35. Split the 5mg into 4 pieces and you have 392 doses.

Never heard of anyone actually being prescribed individual 1mg tablets here. Pretty much all doctors prescribe the 5mg option and tell them to split it in 4

62352787
>>62350565
no, it is also cheap in the US. it’s just annoying that you can’t buy it with an HSA. it’s also annoying that you need a prescription. should be OTC.

62355508
>>62350624
>>62351707
Literally everyone develops watery semen on it, have fun taking that poison that will fuck you up after some years, instead of just getting a toupe.

62355814
>>62355508
I've taken it for 4 years and never experienced that. Looked it up now and in clinical trials it was a side-effect that hit about 3% of users, and which went back to normal when they discontinued usage.

Tens of millions of men are on finasteride and like 95% of guys who go on the internet to talk about it are doing so because they had negative effects from it and need advice. It's like going to a subreddit about sexual assault and assuming that just because 90% of the people posting there got raped means that 90% of people in real life have been raped as well, when that is obviously far from true

62355979
>>62350349
*repellent for all women

62356219
The mistake most men make when jumping on finasteride is not getting their testosterone and DHT levels checked first. Since using a drug that nukes your DHT while having low T and by extension low DHT is a recipe for disaster.

Like 20% of men between 18-35 have clinically low T (under 300ng/dl) that warrants medical treatment. Up to 30% are in the danger zone (under 350ng/dl) with mild symptoms of low T. Any doctor who prescribes finasteride to these people and nuking their DHT levels even further deserve to lose their license for malpractice

62360477
